title: JLPT N3: Fuji Registration Rules
passage: fujisan ni noboru hito ga oosugirunode, yamanashiken wa atarashii ruuru wo tsukurimashita. Gogo yojikara tsuginohino gozen sanji madewa, tozandou wo tooru koto ga dekimasen.
translation: Prefektur Yamanashi membuat aturan baru untuk pendakian Gunung Fuji. Jalur pendakian ditutup dari jam 4 sore hingga jam 3 pagi.

questions.question: Why did Yamanashi prefecture create new rules?
When is the climbing path closed?
options: From 4 pm to 3 am next day

questions.question: What fee must climbers pay?

title: JLPT N2: Japanese Environmental Policy
passage: Japan has committed to achieving carbon neutrality by 2050 as part of its Green Growth Strategy. The government is investing heavily in hydrogen energy, offshore wind power, and next-generation solar cells. This transition requires collaboration between industry, academia, and government to develop innovative technologies.
translation: Jepang berkomitmen mencapai netralitas karbon pada 2050. Pemerintah berinvestasi dalam energi hidrogen, angin lepas pantai, dan sel surya generasi berikutnya.

questions.question: By when has Japan committed to carbon neutrality?
Which energy type is mentioned in Japan Green Growth Strategy?
What collaboration does the transition require?

title: JLPT N4: Japanese Train System
passage: Japan train system is one of the most punctual in the world. Trains arrive and depart within seconds of their scheduled time. The Shinkansen bullet train can reach speeds of over 300 kilometers per hour, connecting major cities efficiently. IC cards like Suica and PASMO make traveling very convenient.
translation: Sistem kereta Jepang sangat tepat waktu. Shinkansen mencapai kecepatan lebih dari 300 km/jam, menghubungkan kota-kota besar. Kartu IC seperti Suica dan PASMO memudahkan perjalanan.

questions.question: What is Japan train system known for?
options: Being the fastest in the world
Being the most punctual in the world

questions.question: How fast can Shinkansen travel?
What are Suica and PASMO?

title: JLPT N5: Daily Life in Japan
passage: In Japan, people follow many daily customs. They remove their shoes before entering a home. They bow when greeting someone as a sign of respect. Trains and buses are very quiet and passengers do not talk on the phone. People separate garbage into different categories for recycling.
translation: Di Jepang, orang melepas sepatu sebelum masuk rumah, membungkuk saat menyapa, menjaga ketenangan di transportasi umum, dan memisahkan sampah untuk daur ulang.

questions.question: What do people in Japan do before entering a home?
What is bowing used for in Japan?
How do Japanese people handle garbage?

title: JLPT N3: Japanese School System
passage: Japanese students attend school six days a week and have very demanding schedules. Elementary school lasts six years, junior high school three years, and high school three years. After high school, many students prepare intensively for university entrance examinations. Club activities after school are important for developing social skills.
translation: Siswa Jepang sekolah 6 hari seminggu. SD 6 tahun, SMP 3 tahun, SMA 3 tahun. Banyak yang mempersiapkan ujian masuk universitas secara intensif. Kegiatan klub penting untuk pengembangan sosial.

questions.question: How many days per week do Japanese students attend school?
How many years does elementary school last in Japan?
What are club activities important for?

title: JLPT N4: Japanese Food Culture
passage: Japanese cuisine, known as washoku, was recognized as UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage in 2013. It emphasizes fresh seasonal ingredients, beautiful presentation, and umami flavor. Popular dishes include sushi, ramen, tempura, and udon. The Japanese tradition of saying itadakimasu before eating shows gratitude for the food.
translation: Masakan Jepang (washoku) diakui sebagai Warisan Budaya Tidak Benda UNESCO pada 2013. Menekankan bahan segar musiman, penyajian indah, dan rasa umami. Tradisi itadakimasu menunjukkan rasa syukur.

questions.question: When was washoku recognized as UNESCO heritage?
What does Japanese cuisine emphasize?
What does saying itadakimasu before eating show?

title: JLPT N3: Japanese Work Culture
passage: Japan has long been known for its intense work culture, where employees often stay long after official working hours end. The concept of karoshi, or death from overwork, became a serious social issue in the 1980s. In recent years, the Japanese government has implemented work style reforms encouraging shorter hours, remote work, and mandatory paid leave usage.
translation: Budaya kerja Jepang dikenal intensif dengan kerja lembur. Konsep karoshi (mati karena kelebihan kerja) menjadi isu sosial. Pemerintah menerapkan reformasi gaya kerja untuk mendorong jam yang lebih pendek dan cuti berbayar.

questions.question: What is karoshi?
When did karoshi become a serious social issue?
What does the Japanese government encourage in work style reforms?
options: Shorter hours, remote work, and paid leave

title: JLPT N5: Seasons in Japan
passage: Japan has four clear seasons. Spring is famous for cherry blossoms called sakura. Summer is hot and humid, with many festivals. Autumn brings beautiful red and orange leaves called koyo. Winter is cold and some areas get heavy snowfall. Japanese people celebrate each season with special foods and activities.
translation: Jepang memiliki 4 musim yang jelas. Musim semi terkenal dengan sakura, musim panas panas dan lembab, musim gugur dengan koyo, dan musim dingin dengan salju di beberapa daerah.

questions.question: What is Japan spring famous for?
What is koyo?
How does Japan summer climate feel?

title: JLPT N2: Japanese Technology Innovation
passage: Japan has been a global leader in technological innovation for decades. Companies like Sony, Toyota, and Panasonic revolutionized consumer electronics and automotive industries. Japan also pioneered robotics technology, with industrial robots widely deployed in manufacturing. Currently, Japan is investing in AI and quantum computing research to maintain its technological edge.
translation: Jepang menjadi pemimpin global dalam inovasi teknologi. Sony, Toyota, dan Panasonic merevolusi elektronik dan otomotif. Jepang kini berinvestasi dalam AI dan komputasi kuantum.

questions.question: Which Japanese company is mentioned in relation to automotive industry?
What technology did Japan pioneer?
What is Japan currently investing in?

title: JLPT N4: Japanese Onsen Culture
passage: Hot spring baths called onsen are an important part of Japanese culture. Japan has thousands of onsen resorts throughout the country, utilizing the natural geothermal heat from volcanic activity. Visiting onsen is considered relaxing and beneficial for health, helping with skin conditions, muscle pain, and stress. Traditional inns called ryokan often include onsen facilities.
translation: Onsen (pemandian air panas) adalah bagian penting budaya Jepang. Ribuan resor onsen memanfaatkan panas geotermal. Mengunjungi onsen dianggap bermanfaat bagi kesehatan. Penginapan tradisional ryokan sering menyertakan fasilitas onsen.

questions.question: What are onsen?
What natural source heats onsen?
What are ryokan?
options: Traditional inns that often include onsen
